app.description: Fully automated Cloudflare Tunnel using the Cloudflare API — no dashboard setup, no manual DNS, no token pasting. On first run, the init container creates a locally-managed tunnel, adds wildcard DNS records for every DOMAINNAME_N in your .env, and writes the cloudflared config. Set it and forget it.

variables.name: CLOUDFLARE_TUNNEL_NAME
variables.description: Name for the tunnel in Cloudflare Zero Trust (e.g. homelab, plexy). Used only on first run.

dialog.title: API Token Scope Required
dialog.message:
Create a Cloudflare ACCOUNT API token (not a User API token) with TWO permissions:

  Zone → DNS → Edit
  Account → Cloudflare One Connectors → Edit

Account API Tokens are found at:
dash.cloudflare.com → Manage Account → API Tokens

The init container will create the tunnel and DNS records automatically.

messages.text: Cloudflare Tunnel is running. Wildcard DNS records (*.domain) and ingress rules were created automatically for all DOMAINNAME_N defined in your .env.
To add a new domain: add DOMAINNAME_N to .env, then run: docker compose up -d --force-recreate cloudflare-tunnel-auto-init cloudflare-tunnel-auto
Cloudflare enforces a 100 MB request body limit on proxied traffic. For large file transfers (Nextcloud, Immich) or live video streams (Frigate), use grey-cloud DNS records that bypass the proxy.
To verify: docker logs cloudflare-tunnel-auto-init && docker logs cloudflare-tunnel-auto | grep Registered
